svg.aria-label: § 7.1.1 CGGTY Scheduler Decision Flow — warp-pool snapshot, 4-step pipeline, strategy comparison, cycle timeline

g.text: 1 insn/cyc · 0-cyc context switch · 41 bits/warp software state · W3/W0 ratio = 3.96 (expected 4.0)
Per-cycle hardware logic — applied by each SMSP scheduler in parallel
For every warp w: stall[w] = max(0, stall[w] − 1)
GREEDY: continue last warp if eligible
YOUNGEST: pick min warpID among eligible
In our example: would pick W0 (if step 3 missed)
issue insn → SMSP backend (FMA/ALU/SFU/LSU/TC) · update i-buffer · set new stall
8-cycle dispatch trace — green = greedy continuation, orange = youngest tiebreak
In long-running production kernels (1000+ cyc), greedy continuation dominates: every 4 issues, 3.96 stay on the same warp [S11/c28].
